title: lithocarpus cottonii
intro: Lithocarpus cottonii, also known as Cottonwood Tanbark Oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its tannin content. The health benefits of Lithocarpus cottonii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Lithocarpus cottonii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used in folk remedies and traditional medicine.
